metadata.title: Why a 50-round agent survives past its own memory limit
metadata.topic: Compacting growing context in long agent sessions
metadata.purpose: A compaction directive makes the model emit a summary block for removed content, then continue on the compressed context as if it had been there all along.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: other edit variants, cache/TTL interaction, billing for compaction tokens
metadata.visual_object: A vertical stack of content blocks accumulating until a compaction boundary fires and collapses them into one summary block, with a token counter shown before and after.
metadata.hook: Your agent has run 50 tool calls and the context window is nearly full — truncate and drop history, or compact and keep the gist?

beats.narration_text: A coding agent runs 50 rounds of tool calls across a 2-hour session; context approaches the token ceiling mid-task.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Context grows monotonically with every message and tool result — how does a long agent stay alive without losing the thread? Truncating drops information; unlimited accumulation breaks the model.
Your agent has run 50 tool calls and the context window is nearly full — truncate and drop history, or compact and keep the gist?
A compaction directive makes the model emit a summary block for removed content, then continue on the compressed context as if it had been there all along.
beats.visual: A vertical stack of content blocks accumulating until a compaction boundary fires and collapses them into one summary block, with a token counter shown before and after.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: 50 rounds ≈ 40,000 tokens. Compaction fires at 80% capacity, compressing them into one ≈2,000-token summary. Round 51 begins with 3,000 tokens of live context instead of hitting the ceiling.
A compaction directive makes the model emit a summary block for removed content, then continue on the compressed context as if it had been there all along.
